---
title: pandas
---

```{r, code=readLines("_knitr_config.R"), echo=FALSE}
```
```{css, code=readLines("_style.css"), echo=FALSE}
```

*********************************************************************

> Python Data Analysis Library

*********************************************************************

https://pandas.pydata.org/

*********************************************************************

## installation

```sh
conda install pandas
```

```{python, eval=TRUE}
import pandas as pd
pd.__version__
```

*********************************************************************

## data structures

### Series

> a one-dimensional labeled array capable of holding any data type

### DataFrame

> a two-dimensional labeled data structure with columns of potentially different types

*********************************************************************

## creation


```python

```

*********************************************************************

## indexing

*********************************************************************

## importing

### read_csv

### read_sql

```python
read_sql(query, con)
```

*********************************************************************

Create from scratch, lists, NumPy arrays, dicts, series or other data frames
.shape, len(), .index, .count, .columns, .columns.values
.loc, .iloc, .at, .iat, .ix
set_index to use a column as an index
use column as the index df['colname'] = df.index
the general recommendation is that you use .loc to insert rows in your DataFrame
drop
.drop_duplicates
.rename
. replace
.map
.apply
.applymap
read_csv, parse_dates
.to_csv, .to_excel
pivot, pivot_table, stack, unstack, melt
.iterrows
